enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: If she _______ (study) harder, she would have passed the exam.
questions.reponse: If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam.

questions.texte: The students _______ (tell) to wait outside the principal's office.
questions.reponse: The students were told to wait outside the principal's office.

questions.texte: By the time we arrive, they _______ (already / finish) the meeting.
questions.reponse: By the time we arrive, they will have already finished the meeting.

questions.texte: She said she _______ (be) to London several times.
questions.reponse: She said she had been to London several times.

questions.texte: Recycling _______ (help) protect the environment for future generations.
questions.reponse: Recycling helps protect the environment for future generations.

enonce.texte: Choose the correct modal or conditional form to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: You _______ (must / may) wear a helmet while riding a motorcycle. It's the law.
questions.reponse: You must wear a helmet while riding a motorcycle. It's the law.

questions.texte: If I _______ (was / were) you, I would accept the job offer.
questions.reponse: If I were you, I would accept the job offer.

questions.texte: The government _______ (could / should) invest more in renewable energy sources.
questions.reponse: The government should invest more in renewable energy sources.

questions.texte: If you take the vaccine, you _______ (will / would) be better protected against the disease.
questions.reponse: If you take the vaccine, you will be better protected against the disease.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct word form.

questions.texte: The _______ (responsible) of the citizens is to vote in elections.
questions.reponse: The responsibility of the citizens is to vote in elections.

questions.texte: The government has launched a _______ (prevent) campaign against drug abuse.
questions.reponse: The government has launched a prevention campaign against drug abuse.

questions.texte: The _______ (pollute) of rivers is a serious environmental problem.
questions.reponse: The pollution of rivers is a serious environmental problem.

questions.texte: Many young people are _______ (migrate) to the cities in search of better opportunities.
questions.reponse: Many young people are migrating to the cities in search of better opportunities.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences in reported speech.

questions.texte: "I will apply for the job," she said.
questions.reponse: She said that she would apply for the job.

questions.texte: "Have you completed the assignment?" the teacher asked.
questions.reponse: The teacher asked if I had completed the assignment.

questions.texte: "Don't forget to submit your application," the manager told us.
questions.reponse: The manager told us not to forget to submit our application.

enonce.texte: Transform the following sentences from active to passive voice.

questions.texte: The government has launched a new health campaign.
questions.reponse: A new health campaign has been launched by the government.

questions.texte: People should reduce plastic waste.
questions.reponse: Plastic waste should be reduced.

questions.texte: The Mayor announced the new recycling program.
questions.reponse: The new recycling program was announced by the Mayor.

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions that follow.

Text: Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) is a harmful practice that is still prevalent in some communities. The perpetrators of this act often claim cultural or religious reasons. However, the practice has severe consequences for the health and well-being of girls and women. Governments and NGOs are working together to raise awareness and put an end to this violation of human rights.

questions.texte: Identify the main topic of this text.
questions.reponse: The main topic is Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) and the efforts to end this harmful practice.

questions.texte: Why do perpetrators claim to practice FGM?
questions.reponse: The perpetrators often claim cultural or religious reasons for practicing FGM.

questions.texte: What are the consequences of FGM mentioned in the text?
questions.reponse: The text mentions that FGM has severe consequences for the health and well-being of girls and women.

questions.texte: Explain the role of governments and NGOs in addressing this issue.
questions.reponse: Governments and NGOs are working together to raise awareness and put an end to this violation of human rights.

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions.

Text: Soil erosion is a significant environmental problem that affects agricultural productivity. When topsoil is lost, it takes nutrients with it, reducing the fertility of the land. Farmers can combat soil erosion by planting trees, building windbreaks, and practicing no-till farming. These conservation methods help maintain soil health and sustain food production.

questions.texte: What is the main problem described in the text?
questions.reponse: The main problem described is soil erosion.

questions.texte: How does soil erosion affect the land?
questions.reponse: Soil erosion reduces the fertility of the land by taking nutrients with the topsoil.

questions.texte: Mention three methods farmers can use to combat soil erosion.
questions.reponse: Farmers can combat soil erosion by planting trees, building windbreaks, and practicing no-till farming.

questions.texte: Explain the relationship between soil erosion and food production.
questions.reponse: Soil erosion reduces land fertility, which decreases agricultural productivity. This can lead to reduced food production and food insecurity.

enonce.texte:
You recently purchased a product online that was faulty. Write a formal email of complaint to the customer service department.

Your email should include: the product purchased, the date of purchase, the problem with the product, and what you expect them to do.

questions.texte: Write a formal email of complaint (150-200 words).
questions.reponse:
Subject: Complaint about faulty product - Order #12345

Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to formally complain about a product I recently purchased from your online store. On the 15th of January, I ordered a laptop (Model XYZ). I received it on the 20th of January, but unfortunately, the screen stopped working after two days of use. I am very disappointed with this product.

I would like to request a full refund or a replacement for this faulty item. Please inform me about the procedure to return the product and obtain a refund.

I look forward to your prompt response.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]

[Your Contact Information]

enonce.texte: As the senior prefect of your school, you have been asked to deliver a speech during the morning assembly about the issue of drug consumption among students.

questions.texte: Write a speech (180-250 words) that includes the causes, consequences, and solutions to drug consumption.
questions.reponse:
Speech on Drug Consumption

Good morning, fellow students, teachers, and staff.

Today, I stand before you to address a very serious issue that is affecting our school community: drug consumption. This is a problem that threatens our future and our health.

The causes of drug consumption are varied: peer pressure, stress, curiosity, and a desire to escape from problems. Many students begin using drugs without fully understanding the consequences.

The consequences are devastating: health problems, addiction, poor academic performance, and a loss of focus on our goals. Drugs can destroy families and futures.

However, there are solutions. We must raise awareness about the dangers of drugs. We should create support groups where students can talk about their problems. We need to encourage healthy habits like sports and music. We must also report any suspicious activities to our teachers or counselors.

I urge each one of you to make a conscious choice for a healthy life. Together, we can create a drug-free school and a brighter future.

Thank you for your attention.

enonce.texte: A local NGO is looking for volunteers to help with their environmental awareness campaign. Write a cover letter to apply for this position.

questions.texte: Write a cover letter (150-200 words) expressing your interest and qualifications.
questions.reponse:
Subject: Application for Volunteer Position - Environmental Campaign

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the volunteer position for your environmental awareness campaign. I am a student in my final year and I am very passionate about protecting the environment.

I have experience in community service, having participated in clean-up campaigns in my neighborhood. I am organized, hardworking, and able to work in a team. I am also comfortable using social media to raise awareness.

I believe that my skills and enthusiasm will be an asset to your campaign. I am available to start immediately and can dedicate my weekends to this cause.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]

[Your Contact Information]
